general_info.definition: Fluorescent carbon nanoparticles synthesized from tender coconut waste via hydrothermal treatment, exhibiting excellent aqueous solubility and photoluminescence properties.

method.conditions: Carbonization of tender coconut waste, followed by hydrothermal treatment at 180°C for 4 hours

post_treatment: Dialysis (MWCO 3000 Da) against double distilled water for 24 h

optical.fluorescence_lifetime: 2.1, 2.08 and 2.01ns with different concentrations of Ethionamide.

structural.morphology: spherically shaped, smaller in size and well dispersed
structural.crystallinity: a broad diffraction peak suggest that amorphous nature of CDs

applications.performance: Selective detection of Ethionamide (ETA) via fluorescence quenching (Inner Filter Effect). Linear range: 0-2.4 μg mL⁻¹. Limit of Detection (LOD): 0.33 μg mL⁻¹. Recovery in spiked urine and tap water samples: 97.7-101.6%. Relative Standard Deviation (RSD) ~3%.
applications.comparative_advantage: First report of CDs for ETA detection. Method is simple, low-cost, green (uses waste biomass), selective and sensitive

applications.performance: Blue, green, and red fluorescence imaging of Saccharomyces cerevisiae yeast cells. Blue, green, and red emissions observed under 405, 488, and 561 nm excitation respectively.
applications.comparative_advantage: Low cytotoxicity, excellent biocompatibility, multicolor emission capability. Suitable alternative to hazardous semiconductor quantum dots for cell imaging.

feedback.label: Good
